entries.title: Up-to-date popup made explicit; mac workaround install mount selection fixed
entries.details: Manual update checks now show a clear visible popup in login mode (not just subtle inline messaging). The macOS workaround installer now uses the exact mountpoint returned by `hdiutil attach` and cleans up via trap, preventing wrong-volume picks when multiple DMGs are mounted.

entries.title: Added macOS unsigned install workaround script
entries.details: Added `scripts/install-macos-workaround.sh` to automate DMG download, install to /Applications, quarantine removal, and launch for users who do not have Apple notarization in place. Release workflow now publishes this script as a release asset, and README includes copy/paste commands for latest or tagged installs.

entries.title: Added explicit up-to-date popup and reduced surprise Windows restarts
entries.details: Manual update checks now show a clear 'You are up to date' popup when no update is found, and surface update-available feedback directly in login status copy. Updater auto-install-on-quit is now disabled to prevent unexpected close/reopen behavior on Windows when users are not explicitly installing.

entries.title: Updater install handoff hardened after download
entries.details: Restart & install now validates that an update is fully downloaded before attempting install, sets an explicit installing state, and shows clearer UI feedback while relaunch is in progress. This addresses cases where install appeared to do nothing after download.

entries.title: Fixed update-log refresh loop and polished login update controls
entries.details: Resolved an aggressive refresh loop in the login update log by stabilizing refresh callback dependencies and tracking payload in a ref. Login update controls were restyled into a cleaner floating panel with an icon, better visual hierarchy, and friendlier last-check copy (including 'Last checked just now').

entries.title: v1.0.11: macOS notarization pipeline + login last-checked status
entries.details: Release workflow now supports signed/notarized macOS artifacts when Apple credentials are configured in GitHub secrets, preventing repeated 'app is damaged' installs. Login screen update controls now include a bottom-right Check for updates action with a Last checked timestamp.

entries.title: v1.0.9: In-app updater + stronger update log reliability
entries.details: Tactile now supports in-app update flow in packaged builds: check for update, download with progress screen, and restart-to-install. Login update log fetch behavior was hardened with timeout guards, no-cache headers, local cache fallback, request de-duping, and safer visibility polling so notes refresh more consistently when GitHub is slow.

entries.title: Shape vocabulary, chrome & density, column override, layout edit, UI presets
entries.details: Theme menu now separates **Shape & chrome** (Soft / Organic / Era / Sharp corner vocabulary, light–heavy surface chrome on the workspace grid, cozy–spacious density), **Columns & regions** (auto vs forced left/right sidebar with compact–wide widths using `data-ecs-force-sidebar` + CSS `!important` overrides on large screens, plus **Layout edit mode** with drag-and-drop and keyboard-reorder handles for the three main vertical bands: title bar, status strip, campaign+editor grid). Battle character dragging pauses during layout edit to avoid conflicting drag sources. **UI presets** save and restore the full bundle (and optional color scheme snapshot) to `localStorage` under `ecs-ui-presets-v1`, with save / overwrite / apply / delete / reload. Per-account appearance JSON gains `cornerStyle`, `chromeWeight`, `sidebarPlacement`, `sidebarWidth`, `workspaceDensity`, `workspaceFlow`, and `activeUiPresetId`; legacy `useSoftCorners` still migrates on read. Developer Overview + State snapshot list the new fields.

entries.title: Global button UI tick + stronger palette backdrops
entries.details: Optional UI sound now plays on primary mouse/pen presses for real `<button>` elements, `input[type=button|submit|reset]`, and `[role=button]` across the app (capture-phase listener; skips disabled/`aria-disabled`; respects `[data-ecs-no-ui-sound]` on subtrees). Scheme still tints the short triangle tick via Web Audio. CSS backdrops were pushed further apart: Aero meadow (violet) gets a Bliss-style green hill wash plus a dark-night overlay variant; Wii U reads as a warm yellow–cyan plaza with a bottom white burst and soft “bubble” highlights; GameCube deepens the indigo→teal sweep with a stronger lime/violet sparkle layer; XMB adds a rose floor glow under the cyan crown.

entries.title: Palette research pass, CSS era decor, optional chime, Bionicle removed
entries.details: Removed the Bionicle color scheme end-to-end (picker, backdrop layer, CSS, and changelog tones); saved `colorScheme: "bionicle"` in local appearance JSON now migrates to Default on load. Refined scheme blurbs and several gradient stacks toward more period-specific references (Aero Bliss hues, deeper Win98-style blues, Y2K sky wash, XMB night floor, GameCube BIOS indigo, Wii U tile wash, 3DS shell tint). Added pure-CSS `::before` decor layers on each palette backdrop (subtle paper grain, glass sheen, Win98 cloud wash, Y2K nebula, Wii channel bands, XMB floor vignette, Cube sparkle, Wii U grid, 3DS upper-screen gloss). Theme menu includes an off-by-default **Palette chime** toggle: when on, picking a scheme in the Theme grid or Dev Tools plays a short Web Audio blip after your click. Dev Tools state JSON includes `uiSoundsEnabled`.

entries.title: Corner mode fixes, login changelog on shell, glass log panels
entries.details: Corner style (Soft vs Era) now wins over Tailwind utilities using !important on clip-path and radius for ecs-shape-* classes; Era also forces zero border-radius on ecs-shape-banner/card so angled cuts are not doubled with rounded corners. Workspace header (Aero default + Y2K sunset) and login hero (violet/sunset) only attach ecs-shape-banner when Era is on—Soft uses diagonal strip + rounded rect instead. Violet/sunset workspace cards only add ecs-shape-soft when Soft is on. Login changelog is moved out of the inner gradient card so it sits on the same ecs-aero-login-shell backdrop as the page; LoginUpdateLog shells use slightly more translucent blurred fills (default, violet, wii, xmb) so the panel reads as part of the active theme.

entries.title: Header Theme menu, TTRPG/DnD toggle, squarer Win98 chrome
entries.details: Three UX changes. (1) New header **Theme** button opens a dedicated panel (color scheme grid, layout, light/dark, corners, per-account save, append-generated-attacks) so palette is not buried in Settings; Settings now points to the header for theme and rules. (2) **TTRPG** / **DnD** segmented control in the header swaps sheet rules without opening Settings. (3) Late-90s **teal** palette is visually squarer: workspace panels use `rounded-sm` instead of `rounded-3xl`, Win98 header chrome uses square top strip, teal `cardClass` drops `ecs-shape-soft`, and CSS forces classic clip-path on shape cards plus 2px radius on `ecs-shape-soft` under teal so inner DnD tiles match the era.

entries.title: Login shell per palette, manual attacks, merge batches, soft vs era corners, per-account theme
entries.details: Seven changes. (1) The login page `.ecs-aero-login-shell` background now follows Default, Wii U, 3DS, and Bionicle palettes (not only teal/sunset/wii/xmb/cube). Aero meadow / violet intentionally keeps the signature aqua diagonal-strip shell. (2) `data-ecs-corners`: Soft rounds clip-path cards, banners, stat wedges, and portrait hex frames; Era restores the stylized angled shapes for period-accurate chrome. (3) Settings: remember appearance per account on this device (guest theme still saved for the sign-in screen), optional append for generated attacks (unique batch IDs so merges never collide), and reset-to-defaults covers the new toggles. (4) Attack generator always includes all eight archetypes (spell + subtle no longer gated by element tone), and each generate call sends a random batch id for stable unique attack ids. (5) Manual attack entry: name, hit, dice, type, range, description, plus Manual badge in TTRPG and DnD lists. (6) Dev tools state snapshot lists the new appearance flags. (7) `index.html` seeds default `data-ecs-*` attributes to reduce first-paint theme flash.

entries.title: Theme-aware changelog, generic Bionicle, deletable + uncapped attacks, contrast pass
entries.details: Five changes. (1) The login changelog panel now styles itself per active color scheme — the panel chrome, divider, version pill, refresh button, entry cards, dates, titles, body text, and footer all pick up the matching palette so it never looks pasted-on. Default theme renders as a clean light/dark slate panel; Aero is glass + cyan; Win98 is beveled gray with the iconic blue date label; Bionicle is obsidian + gold trim, etc. (2) Bionicle theme is no longer Tahu-specific — base shifted from molten lava + orange accents to obsidian + brushed gunmetal with a gold trim, and the backdrop now glows softly in all six Toa tribe colors (red fire / blue water / green air / brown earth / white ice / gray stone) with elemental-colored motes and a kanohi-mask silhouette ringed by six small element rivets. (3) Every attack now has a Remove button (× in DnD, full Remove in TTRPG), generated attacks are marked with a small 'Gen' badge so you can tell what came from the generator, and attack descriptions render under each one so you don't have to guess what it does. (4) Attack generator no longer caps at 5 — it produces one attack per (archetype × element) pair, so a single keyword yields ~8 differently-shaped attacks (heavy melee, finesse, reach, thrown, ranged, spell, AoE, subtle) and three keywords can yield 20+. (5) Per-theme readability pass: every themed palette now overrides the default muted Tailwind text colors (slate-500/400/700/200) with palette-tinted equivalents inside the workspace shell, so hint text and metadata stay legible on Bionicle's obsidian, XMB's slate, 3DS's rose, etc. without affecting modal portals like Settings or Dev Tools.

entries.title: Bionicle theme, smarter attack generator, real Dev Tools
entries.details: Three big changes. (1) Compact toggles for the character editor and battle cards moved out of Settings and into a small dropdown next to the Sheet/Battle tabs — exactly where you'd expect to change a tab's density. (2) Attack generator rebuilt: instead of three near-duplicate attacks from one keyword, every attack is now a distinct (archetype × element) pair — heavy melee, finesse, reach, thrown, ranged, spell bolt, AoE cone, or subtle/debuff — so a single keyword like 'fire' yields a heavy lava cleave, a fast cinder jab, a pyre cone, a flame spell bolt, etc. with different ranges, dice, ability mods, and full SRD damage-type coverage (fire / cold / lightning / thunder / radiant / necrotic / force / poison / acid / psychic / slashing / piercing / bludgeoning). (3) Dev mode now actually does something: unlocking it adds a 🛠 Dev button to the header that opens a tabbed panel with an Overview (counts, account, current scheme), live State JSON snapshot with copy-to-clipboard, Theme override (color scheme / layout / light-dark / TTRPG-DnD), localStorage browser with clear-and-reload, and a Data tab that seeds six Toa as level-5 sample characters or wipes everything. Plus a brand-new Bionicle (Tahu) color scheme — obsidian black with molten lava-orange accents, gold-edged buttons, drifting embers, a glowing kanohi-mask silhouette, and a slow lava-pool pulse — based on the 2001 Toa Mata fire-tribe palette.

entries.title: App-wide UX cleanup: toolbar, sidebar, battle, and auth
entries.details: Carried the character-creation streamlining into the rest of the app. Toolbar is now grouped (Create / Save+Delete / Generate / Preset) with visual dividers and an icon on every primary action; the three display toggles moved into a new 'Display density' section in Settings with inline explanations. Campaign sidebar drops the 'Show campaign tools' gate — Create new and Join code are always visible as a small two-tab control, the share code has a one-click Copy button, and the active campaign card cleanly stacks share code → members → leave. Battle board got a real header with a Round badge, a single Encounter controls row that highlights 'Next turn' as primary, plus per-character cards that now group HP (with damage/heal steppers and sheet reference) and Combat stats (AC / Init / Faction) into two clearly-labelled blocks. Sidebar character list adds a search icon, clear-search ×, empty-state copy, a count badge, and an always-on grip handle when in battle mode. Auth screen heading + button label now match the active mode (Sign in / Create account / Set new password / Unlock dev mode), 'Forgot password?' is its own link, and 'Dev mode' is a separate toggle under 'Developer options'.

entries.title: Streamlined character creation wizard
entries.details: The 'New character' button now opens a 4-step wizard inspired by Pathfinder/D&D best practices: pick a class or role from visual tiles, name it, tune the stats (HP/AC/level auto-suggested from your pick), then review. Each step has inline guidance, you can jump back to any step from the review, and a 'Quick build' shortcut on step 1 fills in defaults and skips straight to review. The 'Blank sheet' button is still there for power users who want full editor control.

entries.title: Cleaner Settings panel with always-visible help text
entries.details: Settings menu now follows industry-standard form patterns: grouped into Game and Appearance sections, every control has a one-line explanation right below it (no more hover tooltips that may not appear), focus rings are visible, and a 'Reset to defaults' button anchors the footer. The Settings/Logout buttons in the header gained icons (⚙ / ⏻) and a divider so destructive actions sit visually apart. Sheet/Battle is now a real ARIA tablist.

entries.title: Default theme, layout toggle, and settings tooltips
entries.details: New 'Default' clean modern color scheme and a 'Page layout' toggle that lets every theme fall back to the standard sidebar-left workspace. Settings menu now has hover/focus tooltips explaining Mode, Color scheme, Page layout, and Theme. Removed the parchment visual style.

entries.title: Per-theme accuracy & layout polish
entries.details: Aero gets a wider Vista-style nav pane with blue glass wash. Y2K switches to a MySpace-style right widget panel with a '★ My Profile ★' ribbon. Win98 grows a real bottom status bar with bevelled cells. 3DS keeps its dual-screen bezels with a hinge separator. Wii U pins status pills as a system bar; XMB lifts them above the title.

entries.title: Game console color schemes
entries.details: Five new color schemes inspired by classic consoles: Wii channels (silver + rounded tiles), PS3 XMB (animated wave + monthly tints), GameCube BIOS (indigo glass cubes), Wii U dashboard (cyan glass tiles), and Nintendo 3DS (red shell + dual screen).

entries.title: GitHub-hosted update log
entries.details: Login screen pulls live notes from Raw GitHub (see update-feed.config.json). Settings menu renders above all UI via a portal overlay.

entries.title: Login screen & settings polish
entries.details: Update log on sign-in with periodic refresh; settings menu uses a top-layer overlay so it stays visible.

entries.title: Auth & campaign workspace
entries.details: Login-first flow, sessions, campaigns, encounter board, and DnD sheet mode.
